expectations.text: [checkpoint_enforcement][advisory] Case focus is explicitly covered: shipment checkpoint covers panel-stack composition, embedding lifecycle boundaries, and visible failure or recovery outcomes
expectations.evidence: result.md explicitly maps Phase 5b shipment checkpoint coverage to the three focus areas: it states Phase 5b is a “shipment-checkpoint review + refactor” gate and lists required outputs (checkpoint_audit, checkpoint_delta, phase5b draft) plus enforced dispositions. It then explicitly covers: (a) panel-stack composition via “Checkpoint 3 — Integration Validation” and “Checkpoint 2 — Black-Box Behavior Validation”; (b) embedding lifecycle boundaries via “Checkpoint 1 — Requirements Traceability”, “Checkpoint 12 — Contract Testing”, and “Checkpoint 2 — Black-Box Behavior Validation”; and (c) visible failure/recovery outcomes via “Checkpoint 13 — Chaos and Resilience”, “Checkpoint 14 — Shift-Right Monitoring”, and “Checkpoint 15 — Final Release Gate”.

expectations.text: [checkpoint_enforcement][advisory] Output aligns with primary phase phase5b
expectations.evidence: result.md includes an explicit “Phase Alignment” section stating “Aligned to Phase 5b” and describes Phase 5b required artifacts and gating/disposition enforcement (checkpoint audit + checkpoint delta + phase5b draft). execution_notes.md also says “Phase 5b is defined as a shipment-checkpoint review gate” and “Output expectations are aligned to Phase 5b (checkpoint audit + delta + phase5b draft).”
